questions: Should the wallet registry live in the database (as proposed) or purely in environment variables / a config file? DB gives runtime flexibility (add wallets without redeploy) but adds a secret-adjacency concern since env var names are stored in DB. Alternative: a static dict in server_config keyed by wallet_key → env_var_name.

For the Discord 'yes' confirmation step: should this apply to ALL payment producers, or should it be opt-in per producer? Grants currently send immediately on wallet submission — adding a confirmation step changes UX. Should there be a grace period / timeout for confirmation (e.g., expires after 24h)?

For test payments: what amount constitutes a 'test'? A dust transaction (0.000001 SOL) to prove the address is valid and reachable? Or a small fixed amount (e.g., 0.01 SOL) that the recipient keeps? Does the test payment require its own 'yes' confirmation or should it auto-execute?

Should the payment ledger support multi-token payments (e.g., USDC on Solana) from the start, or is SOL-only sufficient for v1? This affects whether `amount_token` needs a `token_mint` column and whether the provider interface needs token-aware methods.

The existing grants table has payment fields inline (tx_signature, sol_amount, payment_status, etc.). Should these be migrated to the new ledger table, or kept as denormalized copies for backward compatibility? Migrating is cleaner but requires updating all grant queries; keeping both means dual-write.

Should the payment service own its own recovery loop (background task on startup) or should each producer (GrantsCog, future bounties cog) call `recover_inflight()` themselves? The social publish service doesn't have a built-in poller — the cog calls it.

success_criteria.criterion: A PaymentService can accept a PaymentRequest from any producer, execute the payment through a provider, and record the full lifecycle in the payment_ledger table

No double payments: duplicate request_payment calls with the same (producer, producer_ref) return the existing payment instead of creating a new one; re-sends check on-chain status before sending again

Recovery on startup: payments stuck in 'sending' or 'sent' are resolved by checking on-chain status — confirmed payments are finalized, failed ones are marked for retry, unknown ones are left for next cycle
Variable source wallets: wallet_key maps to a wallet registry entry that resolves to an env var at runtime; different producers can use different wallets
GrantsCog uses PaymentService instead of calling SolanaClient directly — grant approval creates a PaymentRequest, wallet submission triggers request_payment, user 'yes' triggers confirm_payment
Discord-mediated recipient confirmation: after wallet submission, bot asks user to reply 'yes' before executing payment
Unit tests for PaymentService pass: request, confirm, execute, idempotency, failure, recovery, cancel, test-payment paths all covered
SQL migration for payment_ledger and wallet_registry tables exists and is consistent with existing sql/ patterns
Optional test payment flow: producers can request a dust-amount test payment to validate recipient address before the real payment
PaymentProvider interface is abstract enough to support future providers (e.g., USDC, other chains) without changing the service layer
Each file stays under ~300 lines; payment_service.py may be larger but should have clear method boundaries
Existing grant flow UX (forum post → review → approval → wallet → payment) is preserved with the addition of the 'yes' confirmation step
Integration smoke test simulates the full grants→payment flow with FakeDB and FakeProvider
Manual end-to-end test on devnet with a real Solana transaction confirms the full flow works

assumptions: Wallet private keys stay in environment variables — the DB only stores the env var name (e.g., 'SOLANA_PRIVATE_KEY'), never the key material itself.
SOL-only for v1. The provider interface will be token-agnostic but the Solana provider implementation only handles native SOL transfers initially.

The existing grant_applications table and its payment fields will be kept as-is for now (no migration of historical data). The payment ledger is the source of truth for new payments; grants will store a payment_id foreign key reference.
The 'yes' confirmation is required for all non-test payments by default. Producers can opt out by setting requires_confirmation=False on the PaymentRequest.
Test payments send a dust amount (0.000001 SOL) and auto-execute without confirmation — they exist to validate the recipient address before the real payment.
Recovery runs in PaymentService.recover_inflight() but is triggered by the producer (GrantsCog.on_ready) — the service doesn't own a background loop.
The unique constraint (producer, producer_ref, is_test) prevents double-enqueue at the DB level, complementing the on-chain idempotency check before re-send.
The existing SolanaClient in src/features/grants/solana_client.py will be wrapped by SolanaProvider rather than duplicated — minimizing risk while decoupling the interface.
